subject: [POLICY] Add date to logging timestamp
commit_message:
[POLICY] Add date to logging timestamp

Policy components log the current time to stdout
but not the actual date.   This patch adds the
date in UTC format.

Issue-ID: POLICY-3177
Signed-off-by: jhh <jorge.hernandez-herrero@att.com>
Change-Id: If86cad58115d9124e09a953c0aa807644830ad4b
Signed-off-by: jhh <jorge.hernandez-herrero@att.com>
(cherry picked from commit 417bcbae7b6cdb92da9ab8cb02f7bc31c9f5192d)
(cherry picked from commit 37fba0c3fe0fd169ea05363d532835fa9f7d2959)

going back to guilin with this? Is this a part of maintenance release? Does it make sense?
-1 for visibility

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1
> 
> going back to guilin with this? Is this a part of maintenance release? Does it make sense?
> -1 for visibility

this is for a bug fix that was recently reported in Guilin, long after the maintenance release was planned: https://jira.onap.org/browse/POLICY-3177.  Weren't sure how to handle it, since it's in OOM.

however, it's OK with me if we do not include it in Guilin.  In that case, would you please update the JIRA and explain to fabian that the fix is included in Honolulu and going forward?  If he insists that it needs to be in Guilin, too, then we can continue with this cherry pick.
